name: Environment
summary: An Environment object represents an environment in the model. Environments are used to control the background and lighting of the model. Environments can be used as skydomes, for reflections, and to link the sun to the environment.

methods.name: linked_sun?
methods.summary: The #linked_sun? method is used to determine if the Sketchup::Environment is linked to the sun.
methods.remarks: The #linked_sun? method is used to determine if the Sketchup::Environment is linked to the sun. This function returns a boolean value indicating whether the shadow light feature is currently enabled in the environment. Shadow lighting is used to create realistic shadows in the scene, enhancing the visual quality.

returns.doc: true if the environment is linked to the sun, false otherwise

methods.name: path
methods.summary: The #path method is used to get the file name of the image or SKE file used for the Sketchup::Environment.
methods.remarks: The #path method is used to get the file name of the image or SKE file used for the Sketchup::Environment.

returns.doc: the file name of the image or SKE file used for the environment

methods.name: thumbnail
methods.summary: The #thumbnail method is used to get the thumbnail image of the Sketchup::Environment.
methods.remarks: The #thumbnail method is used to get the thumbnail image of the Sketchup::Environment.

methods.name: use_as_skydome?
methods.summary: The #use_as_skydome? method is used to determine if the Sketchup::Environment is used as a skydome.
methods.remarks: The #use_as_skydome? method is used to determine if the Sketchup::Environment is used as a skydome.

methods.name: use_for_reflections?
methods.summary: The #use_for_reflections? method is used to determine if the Sketchup::Environment is used for reflections.
methods.remarks: The #use_for_reflections? method is used to determine if the Sketchup::Environment is used for reflections.

returns.doc: true if the environment is used for reflections, false otherwise

methods.name: write_hdr
methods.summary: The #write_hdr method writes the HDR, EXR or SKE image of the environment to a file in its original file type.
methods.remarks: The #write_hdr method writes the HDR, EXR or SKE image of the environment to a file in its original file type.

params.name: path
params.doc: the directory where the image should be written

returns.doc: the full path of the written file

throws.when: if the file name is empty.
if the file cannot be written.

properties.name: description
properties.summary: The #description method gets the description for an Sketchup::Environment.
properties.remarks: The #description method gets the description for an Sketchup::Environment.

properties.name: linked_sun
properties.summary: The #linked_sun= method is used to set if the Sketchup::Environment is linked to the sun.
properties.remarks: The #linked_sun= method is used to set if the Sketchup::Environment is linked to the sun. Shadow lighting is used to create realistic shadows in the scene, enhancing the visual quality.

properties.name: linked_sun_position
properties.summary: The #linked_sun_position method is used to get the position of the sun linked to the Sketchup::Environment.
properties.remarks: The #linked_sun_position method is used to get the position of the sun linked to the Sketchup::Environment. The position is a Geom::Point3d where the x must be in range [0.0, 1.0] and y must be in range [-1.0, 1.0].

properties.name: name
properties.summary: The #name method retrieves the name of the Sketchup::Environment.
properties.remarks: The #name method retrieves the name of the Sketchup::Environment. This is the unique internal name of the object which should be used for retrieving the Sketchup::Environment from the model's Sketchup::Environments.

properties.name: reflection_exposure
properties.summary: Note: Reflection exposure is a value between 0.
properties.remarks: Note: Reflection exposure is a value between 0.0 and 10.0, where 0.0 is no exposure and 10.0 is full exposure. The #reflection_exposure method is used to get the exposure of the Sketchup::Environment for reflections.

properties.name: rotation
properties.summary: The #rotation method is used to get the vertical rotation angle in degrees to apply to the Sketchup::Environment.
properties.remarks: The #rotation method is used to get the vertical rotation angle in degrees to apply to the Sketchup::Environment.

properties.name: skydome_exposure
properties.summary: Note: Skydome exposure is a value between 0.
properties.remarks: Note: Skydome exposure is a value between 0.0 and 20.0, where 0.0 is no exposure and 20.0 is full exposure. The #skydome_exposure method is used to get the exposure of the Sketchup::Environment.

properties.name: use_as_skydome
properties.summary: The #use_as_skydome= method is used to set if the Sketchup::Environment is used as a skydome.
properties.remarks: The #use_as_skydome= method is used to set if the Sketchup::Environment is used as a skydome.

properties.name: use_for_reflections
properties.summary: The #use_for_reflections= method is used to set if the Sketchup::Environment is used for reflections.
properties.remarks: The #use_for_reflections= method is used to set if the Sketchup::Environment is used for reflections.
